title: SystemRDL Elaborated Tree
description: Source of truth for the elaborated SystemRDL register-map tree pushed from systemrdl-lsp to the viewer (Decision 9A: codegen in both directions). v0.2 adds lazy-load support: clients that advertise `experimental.systemrdlLazyTree` capability receive a spine envelope (Reg with empty fields[] + loadState='placeholder' + nodeId) and fetch detail via `rdl/expandNode`. Old clients keep getting full trees.

schemaVersion.description: v0.2 added nodeId / loadState / lazy. Bump when the shape changes.

version.description: Per-URI monotonic counter incremented on every successful elaboration. Clients pass `sinceVersion` in subsequent requests; matching versions return `{unchanged: true}` so the LSP skips serialization (TODO-1).

unchanged.description: When true, the response carries no roots — the client's `sinceVersion` matches the cached tree. Always paired with `version`.

lazy.description: When true, Reg nodes may have loadState='placeholder' (and empty fields[]) and the client must call `rdl/expandNode` with the node's `nodeId` to populate them. Servers only set this when the client advertised `experimental.systemrdlLazyTree` capability during initialize. Clients ignorant of this field will see `loadState='loaded'` (or omitted) on every node and full fields arrays — full backward compatibility.

elaboratedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the LSP completed elaboration.

stale.description: True if this tree is the last good elaboration but a newer parse failed (D7).

roots.description: One entry per top-level addrmap discovered in the workspace (Decision 3C).

displayName.description: Human-readable name from the SystemRDL `name` property — distinct from the identifier name.

isBridge.description: True when the addrmap is marked as a bus bridge (SystemRDL clause 9.2). RTL backends use it as a synthesis hint; the viewer surfaces it in tooltips.

loadState.description: When 'placeholder' (lazy mode only), `fields[]` is empty and the client must call `rdl/expandNode` with this reg's `nodeId` to populate it. Omitted or 'loaded' = `fields[]` carries the real data. Only ever 'placeholder' on responses where the envelope has `lazy: true`.

accessWidth.description: Smallest software access slice. When < `width`, the reg is split-access — software touches it in `width / accessWidth` chunks.

accessSummary.description: Convenient roll-up like 'RW', 'RO', 'RW/RO', 'W1C/RO'. Detail per field in fields[].access.

isCounter.description: True when the field's `counter` SystemRDL property is set.

isIntr.description: True when the field's `intr` SystemRDL property is set.

encode.description: Optional enumerated values from `enum` SystemRDL type.

displayName.description: Optional human-readable label from the enum entry's `name` property — distinct from the identifier `name`.

AccessMode.description: SystemRDL access modes. Maps to design-token --rdl-acc-* CSS variables.

HexU64.description: Hex literal with optional underscores for readability, e.g. '0x0000_0010'.

NodeId.description: Stable opaque id assigned depth-first during a single elaboration pass. Format: lowercase base-36 of the visit index. Stable within a (uri, version) pair — clients pass it back to `rdl/expandNode`. Re-elaboration regenerates ids; mismatch on expandNode triggers VersionMismatch (-32002).

SourceLoc.description: Position in source file for click-to-reveal (Week 6 source map).
